name: Caribe Corn Chowder with Roasted Red Pepper C
recipeIngredient: 4 cups Low sodium chicken broth, canned
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the bacon in a large saucepan over medium heat until the fat begins to render, about 4 minutes.
2. Add butter and stir until melted.
3. Add onion, green and red peppers, celery, garlic, thyme, and bay leaf.
4. Saute the vegetables until they are almost tender but not brown, about 8 minutes.
5. Sprinkle flour over the vegetables.
6. Stir for 1 minute to cook the flour.
7. Gradually stir in the broth, scraping up the flour from the bottom of the saucepan.
8. Bring the chowder to a boil.
9. Add corn and sugar. Cover the saucepan and reduce heat to medium-low.
10. Simmer until vegetables are tender, about 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
11. Stir in cream and season with salt and pepper to taste.
12. Transfer 1-1/2 cups of chowder to a blender and puree.
13. Mix the puree back into the chowder.
14. Simmer for 1 to 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
15. Ladle into bowls and top with a dollop of roasted red pepper cream.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.
